speaker: WILLIAMS
text: I warrant it is to knight you, captain.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: God’s will and His pleasure,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: captain, I beseech you now, come apace to the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: King. There is more good toward you peradventure

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: than is in your knowledge to dream of.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Sir, know you this glove?

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Know the glove? I know the glove is a glove.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: I know this, and thus I challenge it.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: ’Sblood, an arrant traitor as any ’s in the

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: universal world, or in France, or in England!

speaker: GOWER
text: How now, sir? You villain!

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Do you think I’ll be forsworn?

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Stand away, Captain Gower. I will give treason

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: his payment into plows, I warrant you.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: I am no traitor.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: That’s a lie in thy throat.—I charge you in

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: his Majesty’s name, apprehend him. He’s a friend

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: of the Duke Alençon’s.

speaker: WARWICK
text: How now, how now, what’s the matter?

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: My Lord of Warwick, here is, praised be

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: God for it, a most contagious treason come to

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: light, look you, as you shall desire in a summer’s

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: day.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Here is his Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How now, what’s the matter?

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: My liege, here is a villain and a traitor, that,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: look your Grace, has struck the glove which your

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Majesty is take out of the helmet of Alençon.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: My liege, this was my glove; here is the fellow

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: of it. And he that I gave it to in change promised to

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: wear it in his cap. I promised to strike him if he did.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: I met this man with my glove in his cap, and I have

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: been as good as my word.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Your Majesty, hear now, saving your Majesty’s

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: manhood, what an arrant, rascally, beggarly,

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: lousy knave it is. I hope your Majesty is pear me

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: testimony and witness and will avouchment that

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: this is the glove of Alençon that your Majesty is give

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: me, in your conscience now.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Give me thy glove, soldier.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Look, here is the fellow of it.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: ’Twas I indeed thou promised’st to strike,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And thou hast given me most bitter terms.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: An please your Majesty, let his neck answer

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: for it, if there is any martial law in the world.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How canst thou make me

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: satisfaction?

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: All offenses, my lord, come from the heart.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Never came any from mine that might offend your

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: It was ourself thou didst abuse.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Your Majesty came not like yourself. You

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: appeared to me but as a common man; witness the

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: night, your garments, your lowliness. And what

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: your Highness suffered under that shape, I beseech

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: you take it for your own fault and not mine, for, had

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: you been as I took you for, I made no offense.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: Therefore, I beseech your Highness pardon me.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here, uncle Exeter, fill this glove with crowns

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And give it to this fellow.—Keep it, fellow,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And wear it for an honor in thy cap

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Till I do challenge it.—Give him the crowns.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And, captain, you must needs be friends with him.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: By this day and this light, the fellow has

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: mettle enough in his belly.—Hold, there is twelvepence

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: for you, and I pray you to serve God and keep

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: you out of prawls and prabbles and quarrels and

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: dissensions, and I warrant you it is the better for

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: you.

speaker: WILLIAMS
text: I will none of your money.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: It is with a good will. I can tell you it will

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: serve you to mend your shoes. Come, wherefore

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: should you be so pashful? Your shoes is not so

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: good. ’Tis a good silling, I warrant you, or I will

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: change it.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now, herald, are the dead numbered?

speaker: HERALD
text: Here is the number of the slaughtered French.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What prisoners of good sort are taken, uncle?

speaker: EXETER
text: Charles, Duke of Orléans, nephew to the King;

speaker: EXETER
text: John, Duke of Bourbon, and Lord Bouciqualt.

speaker: EXETER
text: Of other lords and barons, knights and squires,

speaker: EXETER
text: Full fifteen hundred, besides common men.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This note doth tell me of ten thousand French

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That in the field lie slain. Of princes in this number

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And nobles bearing banners, there lie dead

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: One hundred twenty-six. Added to these,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Of knights, esquires, and gallant gentlemen,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Eight thousand and four hundred, of the which

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Five hundred were but yesterday dubbed knights.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So that in these ten thousand they have lost,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: There are but sixteen hundred mercenaries.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The rest are princes, barons, lords, knights, squires,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And gentlemen of blood and quality.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The names of those their nobles that lie dead:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Charles Delabreth, High Constable of France;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Jacques of Chatillon, Admiral of France;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The Master of the Crossbows, Lord Rambures;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Great Master of France, the brave Sir Guichard Dauphin;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: John, Duke of Alençon; Anthony, Duke of Brabant,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The brother to the Duke of Burgundy;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And Edward, Duke of Bar. Of lusty earls:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Grandpré and Roussi, Faulconbridge and Foix,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Beaumont and Marle, Vaudemont and Lestrale.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here was a royal fellowship of death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where is the number of our English dead?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Edward the Duke of York, the Earl of Suffolk,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sir Richard Ketly, Davy Gam, esquire;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: None else of name, and of all other men

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But five and twenty. O God, thy arm was here,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And not to us, but to thy arm alone

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ascribe we all! When, without stratagem,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But in plain shock and even play of battle,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Was ever known so great and little loss

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: On one part and on th’ other? Take it, God,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For it is none but thine.

speaker: EXETER
text: ’Tis wonderful.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, go we in procession to the village,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And be it death proclaimèd through our host

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To boast of this or take that praise from God

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Which is His only.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Is it not lawful, an please your Majesty, to

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: tell how many is killed?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yes, captain, but with this acknowledgment:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That God fought for us.

speaker: FLUELLEN
text: Yes, my conscience, He did us great good.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do we all holy rites.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let there be sung Non nobis, and Te Deum,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The dead with charity enclosed in clay,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And then to Calais, and to England then,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where ne’er from France arrived more happy men.
